title: DungeonQuest - Can We Survive Dragonfire Castle? (Play Through)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hhwrtf3g0yY

[Summary]

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes the FFG revised edition of DungeonQuest for a chaotic solo spin using dice-variant combat rules and fan expansions. His character, Chelara—a capable adventurer with strong agility—embarks on a hilariously lethal crawl through Dragonfire Castle. The ruleset is gloriously swingy: drawing dungeon tiles from the Crown Royale bag, testing armor against traps, engaging in simple but brutal combat where a d6 roll determines everything. Daniel's run includes dead adventurers who gift scorpion wounds, crossfire traps narrowly escaped, a giant naga chasing him through catacombs, a bridge that nearly claims him, a minotaur AI opponent stalking the halls, and—just when he's escaping with treasure—a spear trap impales him before the exit. The beauty of DungeonQuest lies in its ability to generate the kind of memorable catastrophe that makes solo gaming sing: one moment you're descending into the dragon's chamber, the next you're hopelessly lost in darkness, rotating chambers, and desperate catacombs scrambles. Daniel's tactical choices matter (when to press luck, when to search, when to escape), but so does the viciously random deck. It's a game that laughs at you while you laugh at it.

----

Have you played DungeonQuest solo, and if so, did you end up escaping with treasure or falling to a spear trap at the last moment?
